findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "generate_authorization_scope" references sensitive artifacts (authorization). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Use this tool when an agent commerce workflow needs a well-formed delegated authorization scope before issuing a signed verification record. It creates financia…

labels.name: generate_authorization_scope
labels.description: Use this tool when an agent commerce workflow needs a well-formed delegated authorization scope before issuing a signed verification record. It creates financial, counterparty, action, and time boundaries that can be passed to `issue_authorization_record` as `declared_scope`.

labels.name: validate_agent_commerce_readiness
labels.description: Use this tool when reviewing a commerce architecture, API marketplace, paid MCP tool, x402 endpoint, or autonomous checkout flow. It checks whether the design uses FLINT as the transaction-time authority layer before execution, verifies signed records, submits outcomes, publishes a Trust Manifest, a

labels.name: issue_agent_passport
labels.description: Use this tool to issue a free FLINT Agent Passport: a hybrid-signed, verifiable identity credential for an autonomous agent. The zero-config mint path only needs agent.agent_name or agent.agent_id. Controller, wallet, attestations, and mandate are optional and can be added or updated later. The pass

labels.name: get_agent_passport
labels.description: Use this tool to resolve a public FLINT Agent Passport by passport_id. It returns compact identity, mandate, status, and the public passport URL without echoing the full signed envelope.

labels.name: update_agent_mandate
labels.description: Use this tool to update mutable mandate config for an existing FLINT Agent Passport. This updates allowed actions, amount limits, or notes only. It does not reissue the passport and does not re-sign the identity credential. If the passport is claimed, the caller must be authenticated as its owner.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: get_agent_passport · Private data: issue_authorization_record, verify_agent_authority, create_flint_trust_manifest, generate_authorization_scope, validate_agent_commerce_readiness, issue_agent_passport, update_agent_mandate · Public sink: submit_transaction_outcome.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: get_agent_passport · Private data: issue_authorization_record, verify_agent_authority, create_flint_trust_manifest, generate_authorization_scope, validate_agent_commerce_readiness, issue_agent_passport, update_agent_mandate · Public sink: submit_transaction_outcome

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
